channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The Latest: VP candidates Vance and Walz meet in last scheduled debate for 2024 tickets
item.description: Barring a late addition to the schedule, Tuesday night's vice presidential debate between J.D. Vance and Tim Walz could be the last debate of the 2024 campaign between the Republican and Democratic tickets.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Some Carter Co. communities 'completely cut off'
item.description: CARTER COUNTY, Tenn. (WJHL) — Parts of Carter County are still without communication as several people remain missing after historic flooding hit the region. On Monday morning, Carter County leaders held a press conference to provide updates on the county's status after the floods.
item.dc:creator: Anslee Daniel, NewsNation

item.title: John Amos, patriarch on 'Good Times' and an Emmy nominee for the blockbuster 'Roots,' dies at 84
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — John Amos, who starred as the family patriarch on the hit 1970s sitcom “Good Times" and earned an Emmy nomination for his role in the seminal 1977 miniseries “Roots,” has died. He was 84. He died Aug. 21 of natural causes in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Attorney says 120 accusers allege sexual misconduct against Sean 'Diddy' Combs
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — An attorney said Tuesday he is representing 120 accusers who have come forward with sexual misconduct allegations against Sean “Diddy” Combs, the hip-hop mogul who is awaiting trial on sex trafficking charges.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Is the food in the fridge still good? California wants to end the guessing game
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California wants to help end the everyday household debate over whether the food in the fridge is still good to eat. Food labels that say “sell by” or “best before” are misleading because they have no universal meaning under current laws.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mississippi justices reject latest appeal from man on death row since 1976
item.description: JACKSON, Miss. (AP) — The Mississippi Supreme Court on Tuesday unanimously denied the latest appeal from a man who has been on the state's death row longer than any other inmate.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Helene's death toll passes 150 as crews search for survivors in North Carolina's mountains
item.description: Cadaver dogs and search crews trudged through knee-deep muck and debris on Tuesday looking in the mountains of western North Carolina for victims of Hurricane Helene, days after the storm carved a deadly and destructive path through the Southeast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hair found in truck helps police identify suspect in 1995 disappearance of Morgan Nick, 6
item.description: ALMA, Ark. (AP) — Hair found in a truck once owned by an Arkansas man who died in prison more than two decades ago was the clue investigators needed to connect the dead inmate to the disappearance of a 6-year-old girl who went missing in 1995.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DEMILLO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Key swing state faces 'daunting' level of uncertainty after storm ravages multiple counties
item.description: North Carolina election officials say they will do everything in their power to ensure that voters in the crucial presidential swing state will be able to cast their ballots despite the devastation of Hurricane Helene only about a month before the November election.
item.dc:creator: Ali Swenson, Christine Fernando And Jeffrey Collins, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Iran fires dozens of missiles into Israel in a sharp escalation of regional conflict
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Iran launched at least 180 missiles into Israel on Tuesday, the latest in a series of escalating attacks in a yearslong conflict between Israel and Iran and its Arab allies that threatens to push the Middle East closer toward a regionwide war.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, MELANIE LIDMAN and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Dockworkers may have the negotiating advantage in their strike against US ports
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — With 45,000 longshoremen at 36 U.S. ports from Maine to Texas on strike for the first time in decades, experts say the workers may wield the upper hand in their standoff with port operators over wages and the use of automation.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER, WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and TASSANEE VEJPONGSA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Claudia Sheinbaum sworn in as 1st female president of Mexico
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Claudia Sheinbaum was sworn in Tuesday as Mexico’s first female president, riding the enthusiasm over her predecessor’s social programs but also facing challenges that include stubbornly high levels of violence.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ and MARIANA MARTÍNEZ BARBA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Florida enacts tough law to get homeless off the streets, leaving cities and counties scrambling
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — As the sun rose Tuesday, Robert Robinson pulled himself from the sidewalk outside Fort Lauderdale's bus depot. It's where he'd slept — and become a violator of Florida's newest law.
item.dc:creator: TERRY SPENCER and KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Justice Department finds Georgia is 'deliberately indifferent' to unchecked abuses at its prisons
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Georgia prison officials are “deliberately indifferent” to unchecked deadly violence, widespread drug use, extortion and sexual abuse at state lockups, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: BY JEFF MARTIN, KEVIN McGILL and ALANNA DURKIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Iran fires dozens of missiles into Israel in a sharp escalation of regional conflict
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Iran launched at least 180 missiles into Israel on Tuesday, the latest in a series of escalating attacks in a yearslong conflict between Israel and Iran and its Arab allies that threatens to push the Middle East closer toward a regionwide war.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, KTLA 5

item.title: Watchdog blasts DEA for not reporting waterboarding, torture by Latin American partners
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — A federal government watchdog is blasting the U.S. Drug Enforcement Administration for failing to timely report human rights violations committed by Latin American law enforcement partners who admitted to waterboarding, suffocating and torturing crime suspects.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge weighs jury instructions in trial of 3 former Memphis officers charged in Tyre Nichols' death
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — A judge was considering arguments Tuesday related to instructions that will be given to jurors in the federal trial of three former Memphis police officers charged with violating Tyre Nichols’ civil rights in a beating that proved fatal following a 2023 traffic stop.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Experienced BASE jumper dies near Utah peak, sheriff says
item.description: Jonathan Bizilia, a 27-year-old BASE jumper, was found dead near Willard Peak in Utah last week after failing to check in with a friend, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why Tuesday's vice presidential debate could matter more than history suggests
item.description: Tim Walz and JD Vance might have the chance to reshape the political landscape Tuesday in their first and only debate.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Iran fires dozens of missiles into Israel in a sharp escalation of regional conflict
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Iran launched at least 180 missiles into Israel on Tuesday, the latest in a series of escalating attacks in a yearslong conflict between Israel and Iran and its Arab allies that threatens to push the Middle East closer toward a regionwide war.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Helene and other storms dumped a whopping 40 trillion gallons of rain on the South
item.description: More than 40 trillion gallons of rain drenched the Southeast United States in the last week from Hurricane Helene and a run-of-the-mill rainstorm that sloshed in ahead of it — an unheard of amount of water that has stunned experts.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN AP Science Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hurricane Helene's death toll passes 150 as crews search for survivors
item.description: Rescuers are scouring the mountains of western North Carolina for anyone still unaccounted for since Hurricane Helene’s remnants caused catastrophic damage to the Southeast.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Collins, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Startling video shows Russian fighter jet flying within feet of US F-16 near Alaska
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — Military officials have released new video of a startling encounter between a Russian fighter jet flying near Alaska and a U.S. Air Force F-16 sent to intercept it. In the video released Monday, the Russian plane comes from behind the camera and swoops by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Baseball legend Pete Rose dies at 83
item.description: Pete Rose, Major League Baseball’s all-time hits leader who endured a lifetime ban for betting on the sport, has died at age 83, his agent Jeff Lenkov confirmed to KTLA 5 News. According to TMZ, Rose died at his home in Las Vegas on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Salt Life is closing all stores across 10 states: See the full list of locations
item.description: Salt Life, a brand synonymous with Florida’s ocean-loving culture, is closing all of its physical stores as part of a significant transition to a wholesale and e-commerce business model.
item.dc:creator: Brody Wooddell, WGN 9

item.title: Trump seeks to put Harris on back foot with Hurricane Helene response
item.description: Former President Trump is seeking to put Vice President Harris on the back foot as both candidates respond to the devastation unleashed by Hurricane Helene. Trump visited Georgia on Monday, where he knocked the federal government’s response to the storm.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Harris says she backs legalizing marijuana, going further than Biden
item.description: Vice President Harris reaffirmed her support for legalizing marijuana Monday, speaking up on the issue publicly for the first time she became the Democratic nominee.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: FBI to pay $22M to settle claims of sexual discrimination at training academy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The FBI agreed Monday to pay more than $22 million to settle a class-action lawsuit alleging female recruits were singled out for dismissal in training and routinely harassed by instructors with sexually charged comments about their breast size, false allegations of infidelity and
item.dc:creator: JIM MUSTIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump suggests ‘1 rough hour’ of policing would end retail crime
item.description: The former president called for law enforcement to be freed up to "do their job" without restrictions.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: What to know about the Walz-Vance debate
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) and Sen. JD Vance (R-Ohio) are set to go head-to-head on Tuesday night in their first and likely only debate as vice presidential contenders.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5

item.title: Trump suspect pleads not guilty in attempted assassination
item.description: The man accused of attempting to assassinate former President Trump at one of his Florida golf courses earlier this month has pleaded not guilty to five federal charges, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: California leads the nation in this lucrative industry
item.description: It's been more than a century since the legendary gold rush of the mid-1800s, and the precious metal is still found in parts of the U.S. today.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, KTLA 5

item.title: Lawmakers fear 'chaos' if Trump-Harris race goes to the wire
item.description: Lawmakers in both parties are bracing themselves for a messy aftermath to Election Day as polls show the race between former President Trump and Vice President Harris is so close in several battleground states that it could take days to determine the winner.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: John Ashton of 'Beverly Hills Cop' dies at 76
item.description: In a career that spanned more than 50 years, Ashton was a regular face across TV series and films, including "Midnight Run,” “Little Big League” and “Gone Baby Gone.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Are professional athletes playing too many games?
item.description: Fans, athletes and medical experts have been having discussions on the increasing demand for players starting from the time they first start playing, all the way up to the professional ranks.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, ABC 4

item.title: Supplies arrive by plane and mule as Helene death toll passes 100
item.description: Desperate residents isolated by washed-out roads and the lack of power and cellular service in western North Carolina lined up Monday for fresh water and a chance to message loved ones that they were alive.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Social Security COLA raise: How much will recipients get in 2025?
item.description: The Social Security Administration is less than two weeks away from announcing its cost of living adjustment (COLA), the annual bump in recipients' payments to help them deal with inflation.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5
